rulings.Case Title: THE GOSEY REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED JANUARY 3, 2007
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner alleges that the principal place of administration has been Escondido, CA. 2) Petitioner requests distribution of $275k to Willie Cole, who has a life estate in real property located at 1119-1121 Cole Street, CA, which has been sold. 3) Trustee fees are being requested at $175/hr. 4) Request for current attorney fees for Trustee is unclear. Par. 10 of the petition states that "$777 has been paid to Carmel & Naccasha LLP" but the accounting does not show such payment. In addition, the invoices attached to the petition from Carmel & Naccasha do not correspond to the $777 amount - there are two invoices: one for $560 and one for $865. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL CARMELO MUTTI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/10/2023 for counsel to address the following: 1. if Creditor's Claims have been filed by C Mutti and by Petitioner. Petition Paragraph #10 states so; however, no claims have been filed with the court. Petitioner needs to provide a proof of his claim. 2. Need to disclose if the creditor is related to the administrator. 3. Need authority under which the court can allow to include the sale proceeds from the ancillary proceeding to the statutory compensation. The proceeds was not subject to this court jurisdiction. Fee base needs to exclude the deposit of the sale proceeds from ancillary proceeding. 4. Need clarification regarding several refunds if the payments were made from the decedent prior to his passing or from the estate funds. If the estate had disbursed the payments, these refunds need to be excluded from the fee base; and 5. if expenses to Lynx Legal needs to considered a part of statutory compensation to attorney or not. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DIANE FRANCES WALSH REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/17/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Court-appointed fiduciaries are required to sign and verify their own pleadings. [CRC 7.103(c).] The only thing an attorney may verify is his own fee declaration. Courtesy copies should just be delivered to the assigned examiner, not attached as an exhibit to another filed pleading. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Issues with a petition must be addressed in either a supplement that provides additional information or an amendment that modifies information already alleged in a petition. [CRC 7.3.] Please review CRC 7.651(b) regarding the description of assets to be distributed to the beneficiaries: individual securities must be set forth, not the lump sum value of all securities to be distributed to each beneficiary. (The attorney's declaration filed on 11/17/22 has not yet been fully reviewed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/6/23. [It is noted that the attorneys for the successor co-trustees previously represented one of the trust beneficiaries. This appears to be a clear conflict. Appearance may be required after all other issues with the accounting and petition are addressed.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KIMBERLY ANNE PUCKETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/17/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] If the statutory fee/commission is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] The date of death is missing from the caption of the Supplemental I&A filed on 7/22/22. The cash reported in the I&A does not appear to have been cash owned by the decedent as of the date of death; Executor reports in paragraph 36 that that amount represents monthly distributions from the limited partnership. Furthermore, the Supplemental I&A has two different amounts on the first page and on Attachment 1. If there was $49,495.26 in cash on hand as of 6/15/22, and $44,335.95 is to be paid to the attorney, how can there be a $10,000 reserve set aside? And how can Decedent's income taxes (not yet filed) be paid from the estate, as alleged in paragraph 28, if there will be no cash left? (Item 4 of the I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee WAS appointed. The husband is not the sole heir, as alleged in paragraph 32; the decedent was also survived by a daughter and the estate reportedly consisted of just separate property. The sisters are neither heirs nor beneficiaries; only the one sister named as the alternate executor needed to be given notice of the petition to administer the estate. The petitioner is not required to give himself "statutory notice" in any event. [Probate Code Sect. 1201.]) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/6/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE R. GENE GEISLER TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 28, 1992, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Probate 850 Petition, For Order Confirming Trust Ownership Of And Conveyance Of Title To Bank Accounts
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: a. Personal Service i. Sara Blake was personally served on 6/22/22 for the 6/28/22 hearing, which was only a 6-day notice. Proof of Service attached to the 11/28/22 Case Status Report shows that she was again served on 10/30/22 for the 12/6/22 hearing date; however, it was not by personal service and was "by other means". ii. Proofs of service attached to the 11/28/22 Case Status Report shows Kattie Blake and Bethany Geisler both being personally served on 10/27/22 for the 12/06/22 hearing date. However, a copy of the petition was not served, and a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served was not attached to the proofs of service, which is needed to verify that the correct information was provided. b. Contents of the safe deposit box has not been described sufficiently. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEGACY ENHANCEMENT POOLED MINOR'S TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Pooled Minor'S Trust; Authorization Of Trustee'S Fees; And Approving Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/10/2023 by the assigned judicial officer after review who directed this tentative ruling to be issued. Papers addressing the following issues must be filed, and courtesy copies of such papers must be delivered, no later than noon on 01/03/2023: 1) Why is venue proper in San Francisco County, rather than Alameda County? The event that gave rise to the minor's claim occurred in Alameda County; the minor and Kaiser are both residents of Alameda County; the proposed trustee is a Texas corporation with its representative located in Pennsylvania; and there is no property of the proposed sub-account located in San Francisco County. If the Court is to approve this petition, the proposed sub-account with the pooled trust will be subject to continuing jurisdiction and supervision of the Court for many years until the minor attains the age of majority. 2) If the Court is to approve the proposed joinder and sub-account with the non-special needs pooled trust as prayed by this petition, what does the petitioner intend to do with Dept. 514's minor's compromise order directing distribution of partial settlement funds to a special needs pooled trust for the minor? 3) What is the initial amount of cash or other assets funding the proposed sub-account? 4) Why should the proposed joinder agreement and master pooled trust be governed by Texas law, rather than California law, even though the situs of the trust will be California, as so stated in the first amendment to the master pooled trust?

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SURVIVOR'S TRUST CREATED UNDER THE JAMES FAMILY TRUST, DATED SEPTEMBER 13, 1973
rulings.Calendar Matter: First Account And Report Of Trustee; Petition For Approval Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/18/2023 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. New notice must be given. 2) No Attachments A1-A3 were filed. 3) A separate schedule must be filed under Probate Code section 1063(e) to show the calculation of interest on the specific cash gifts to be paid. 4) The petitioner became successor trustee more than five years ago. Has the petitioner provided informal annual accountings to the beneficiaries? If not, please explain why the petitioner's first accounting was delayed. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EXEMPT MARITAL TRUST CREATED UNDER THE JAMES FAMILY TRUST, DATED SEPTEMBER 13, 1973
rulings.Calendar Matter: First Account And Report Of Trustee; Petition For Approval Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/18/2023 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. New notice must be given. 2) A copy of the order that admitted the surviving trustor's will to probate must be filed 3) No Attachments A1-A3 were filed. 4) The petitioner became successor trustee more than five years ago. Has the petitioner provided informal annual accountings to the beneficiaries? If not, please explain why the petitioner's first accounting was delayed. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
